stories.headline: Cognition Raises $1 Billion at a $26 Billion Valuation to Scale Its AI Coding Agent
stories.key_takeaway: Cognition more than doubled its valuation to $26 billion in eight months on the strength of its Devin coding agent's surge to a $492 million revenue run rate, betting an independent AI coding company can thrive even as model giants race into the category.

pull_quote.text: More than 90% of the company's internal code is now written by Devin.

stories.article_excerpt: Cognition, the maker of the autonomous AI software engineer Devin, has raised more than $1 billion at a $26 billion post-money valuation...

stories.headline: OpenRouter Raises $113 Million Series B Led by CapitalG to Grow Its AI Model Marketplace
stories.key_takeaway: OpenRouter raised a $113M CapitalG-led Series B at a roughly $1.3 billion valuation as weekly token volume quintupled to 25 trillion, validating the bet that enterprises want a neutral routing layer across a multi-model AI ecosystem.

pull_quote.text: Running inference at scale is fundamentally a multi-model problem. The era of picking a single model is over.

stories.article_excerpt: OpenRouter, the three-year-old startup that has quietly become a switchboard for the AI industry, has raised a $113M...

stories.headline: Supabase Raises $500 Million at a $10.5 Billion Valuation Led by GIC
stories.key_takeaway: Supabase doubled its valuation to $10.5 billion in seven months with a $500M GIC-led Series F, as AI agents like Claude Code now deploy the majority of new databases on its platform.

pull_quote.text: Demand for Supabase is exploding. Our user base has more than doubled since the Series E and we've seen a 600% increase in databases year-over-year. Agents are now deploying the majority of databases on our platform.

stories.article_excerpt: Seven months ago, Supabase was a $5 billion company. As of this month, it is worth more than twice that...

stories.headline: Corgi Insurance Raises $106 Million Series B1 to Build an AI-Native Insurance Platform
stories.key_takeaway: Corgi raised a $106M Series B1 led by TCV at a ~$2.6B valuation, doubling its worth in three weeks as it bets AI-native underwriting can serve startups that legacy carriers underserve.

pull_quote.text: Corgi covers anything from when an AI system causes financial loss, misinformation, operational failures, or compliance issues. Many legacy policies either exclude these risks or handle them ambiguously.

stories.article_excerpt: Corgi, the AI-native insurance startup built for founders, has raised a $106 million Series B1 led by growth investor TCV...

stories.headline: Microsoft Pledges Over $1 Billion to Build AI and Cloud Infrastructure in Thailand
stories.key_takeaway: Microsoft committed over $1 billion through 2028 to build sovereign cloud and AI data center infrastructure in Thailand with major local partners, marking one of its largest Southeast Asia bets.

pull_quote.text: As the cloud and AI become central to economic growth and national competitiveness, countries need both access to world-class technology and the know-how to put it to work.

stories.article_excerpt: Microsoft has committed more than $1 billion to build out cloud and artificial intelligence infrastructure in Thailand...

stories.headline: Microsoft Unveils MAI-Thinking-1 and a Wider Family of In-House AI Models
stories.key_takeaway: Microsoft launched MAI-Thinking-1 and six other in-house MAI models trained from scratch without OpenAI, signaling a strategic shift from dependence on OpenAI to owning its full AI stack.

pull_quote.text: We were only sort of set free from our contract with OpenAI about six months ago to formally pursue superintelligence.

stories.article_excerpt: Microsoft has built its own brain - and trained it without OpenAI's help. At Build 2026, the company introduced MAI...

stories.headline: Anthropic Ships Claude Opus 4.8, Its New Flagship With Effort Control and Dynamic Workflows
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 tops the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index while shipping user-facing effort control, parallel-subagent dynamic workflows, and a 3x cheaper fast mode that reframe how its flagship spends compute.

pull_quote.text: On CursorBench, Claude Opus 4.8 exceeds prior Opus models across every effort level. Tool calling is meaningfully more efficient, using fewer steps for the same intelligence, and it carries end-to-end tasks through.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8, calling its new flagship a modest but tangible improvement that tops rivals...

stories.headline: NVIDIA Unveils Ising, Open-Source AI Models Built to Accelerate Quantum Computing
stories.key_takeaway: NVIDIA's open-source Ising models apply AI to quantum error correction and calibration, claiming up to 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate decoding while extending the company's full-stack grip from GPUs to qubits.

pull_quote.text: AI is essential to making quantum computing practical. With Ising, AI becomes the control plane - the operating system of quantum machines - transforming fragile qubits to scalable and reliable quantum-GPU systems.

stories.article_excerpt: NVIDIA has spent two decades teaching the world that the hard part of computing is not the chip - it is the software...

stories.headline: OpenAI Introduces Deployment Simulation to Replay Old Conversations Through New Models
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's new Deployment Simulation replays roughly 1.3 million real past conversations through a candidate model before release to forecast and catch behavioral regressions, bringing software-style regression testing to AI safety.

pull_quote.text: Deployment Simulation is a method for simulating a future deployment before it happens. We do so by replaying previous conversations in a privacy-preserving manner with a new candidate model.

stories.article_excerpt: For decades, software engineers have run a basic sanity check before shipping new code: replay yesterday's real traffic...

stories.headline: Epoch Releases FrontierMath v2, Rebuilt After Flawed Problems Were Pulled
stories.key_takeaway: Epoch AI's FrontierMath v2 corrected 135 problems and removed 12 after an audit found errors in 42% of the original set, raising scores across the board while leaving model rankings broadly intact.

pull_quote.text: There are easier math benchmarks that are already obsolete, several generations of them. FrontierMath will probably saturate within the next two years - could be faster.

stories.article_excerpt: When a benchmark built by dozens of elite mathematicians turns out to have been wrong about nearly half its questions...

stories.headline: ExpRL Uses Human Reference Solutions to Mid-Train Reasoning Models With On-Policy RL
stories.key_takeaway: ExpRL replaces hand-curated SFT mid-training with on-policy RL that uses hidden human reference solutions as dense reward scaffolds, reaching ~63.41% pass@1 on AIME-2026 versus 58.75% for the strongest baseline.

pull_quote.text: Rather than treating reference solutions as targets to imitate, ExpRL hides them from the policy and uses them only to build problem-specific rubrics, letting an LLM judge reward partial progress and productive reasoning that sparse final-answer signals miss.

stories.article_excerpt: For the past two years, the recipe for a strong reasoning model has been numbingly familiar: pretrain, mid-train...

stories.headline: LoopWM Brings Looped Transformers to World Modeling, Outperforming Far Larger Models
stories.key_takeaway: LoopWM applies looped, iterative-latent-depth transformers to world modeling, reportedly matching or beating models up to ~100x larger at lower compute by making a small model loop instead of scaling parameters.

pull_quote.text: Rather than producing a next-state prediction in a single forward pass, the model refines its latent estimate across multiple loop iterations, spending more thinking depth on harder transitions and trading compute for accuracy at inference without retraining a larger network.

stories.article_excerpt: A new June 2026 arXiv paper argues that the most reliable way to make a world model smarter is not to make it bigger...

stories.headline: EU Publishes First Draft Code of Practice for Labelling AI-Generated Content
stories.key_takeaway: The EU is finalizing a Code of Practice - featuring a proposed common AI icon - to implement Article 50 transparency rules that require labelling of deepfakes, AI text and chatbots from August 2, 2026, with fines up to EUR 15 million or 3% of global turnover.

pull_quote.text: Users need to know they are interacting with AI at the moment of contact - not through a reference buried in fine print.

stories.article_excerpt: Europe is about to make AI-generated content announce itself. As the clock ticks toward the August 2, 2026 deadline...

stories.headline: Japan's Government and Megabanks to Gain Access to Anthropic's Claude Mythos
stories.key_takeaway: Japan's government and reportedly its three megabanks have been granted access to Anthropic's frontier cybersecurity model Claude Mythos, fulfilling a US-Japan Treasury commitment and marking one of the first confirmed sovereign deployments of the tightly gated model.

pull_quote.text: As had been agreed for some time between us and the U.S. Department of the Treasury, the participants include the Government of Japan as well as some Japanese financial institutions.

stories.article_excerpt: Japan has secured a seat at one of the most tightly guarded tables in artificial intelligence. On June 2, 2026...

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches an Economic Research Exchange to Fund Outside Study of AI's Impact
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI launched a grant-and-data program on June 8, 2026, paying outside economists $25,000 plus monthly stipends to study AI's economic impact, raising both data-access opportunities and conflict-of-interest questions about a frontier lab funding research into its own product.

pull_quote.text: Understanding those changes will require more than anecdotes. It will require rigorous empirical research, grounded in real-world evidence and pursued by a broad community of researchers.

stories.article_excerpt: The company that did more than any other to set off the generative-AI boom now wants academic economists to grade its homework.
